heading: Pyth-2 Figure 29 nine-ellipse count reconciled as a source-intended family without upgrading the cattle-answer claim
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, figure29, c5040, source-reconciliation]
body: Reconciled the page-132 `9 different ellipses` claim without promoting the multiple-answer rhetoric. `pyth2_c5040_source_intended_family.py` now treats the source's own nine listed factor pairs as a source-intended family: `(1,2520)`, `(2,1260)`, `(5,504)`, `(7,360)`, `(8,315)`, `(9,280)`, `(35,72)`, `(40,63)`, `(45,56)`. This explains the count directly: it is the validated 8 coprime C=5040 pairs plus one extra source-only noncoprime pair `(2,1260)`. Honest verdict: this repairs the count taxonomy but does not rescue cattle generation. The added ninth pair changes neither the direct-G obstruction nor the slicing-layer obstruction; the source-intended family still yields no integer slice hit for the stabilized bulls packet or its one-seventh lift. The post-cows lane therefore now has 6 stable items and only 3 unresolved items, with the old `5040_count` ambiguity removed and the remaining open questions reduced to the transposition branch, the figure-visibility wording, and the still-unvalidated multiple-answer claim.
